---
title: "small_effect_small_variance"
output: html_document
---

# Power Analysis for Small Effect, Small Variance

```{r}
set.seed(42)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# 1. set effect size and standard deviation
effect_size <- 5
sd <- 15
baseline_mean <- 96
alpha <- 0.05
n_simulations <- 1000 

samples <- seq(20, 500, by = 20)
samples
```

```{r}
# 2. construct power table
power_df <- data.table(
  total_sample_size = samples,
  power = numeric(length(samples))
)
power_df
```
```{r}
# run sim
for (i in seq_along(samples)) {
  
  # Calculate group size for this iteration
  n_group <- samples[i] / 2 
  
  # Simulate the p-values directly
  sim_p_values <- replicate(n_simulations, {
    # Generating rnorm directly avoids costly data.table subsetting
    focus_internal <- rnorm(n_group, mean = baseline_mean, sd = sd)
    focus_external <- rnorm(n_group, mean = baseline_mean + effect_size, sd = sd)
    
    # Run the test and extract the p-value
    t.test(focus_external, focus_internal)$p.value
  })
  
  # Calculate statistical power
  current_power <- mean(sim_p_values < alpha)
  
  set(power_df, i, "power", current_power)
}
```

```{r}
# 4. Visualize the Results
ggplot(power_df, aes(x = total_sample_size, y = power)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "midnightblue", size = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Power Analysis: Internal vs. External Focus",
    subtitle = sprintf("Effect Size = %d | SD = %d | Alpha = %.2f", effect_size, sd, alpha),
    x = "Total Sample Size (Internal + External Groups)",
    y = "Statistical Power",
    caption = "Dashed red line indicates 80% power threshold"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```
